L1.area: Email composition and file attachment handling logic

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled file paths passed to email composition methods can trigger automatic deserialization of untrusted archive metadata via a PHP stream wrapper. This can be exploited to achieve arbitrary object injection when an attacker can influence the file path used for attachments or other file-loading operations within the mailing library.

L3.area: File attachment, embedded content, and file encoding routines within the core mailer class
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled file paths passed to methods that attach files, embed content, or encode file data for emails can trigger PHP's phar stream wrapper, causing automatic deserialization of untrusted phar archive metadata. This allows arbitrary object injection when an attacker can provide or influence the path to a file being attached, embedded, or loaded by the mailer, reintroducing a previously patched object injection issue.

ground_truth.reason: Attacker-controlled file paths passed to attachment and file-handling methods can trigger PHP's phar stream wrapper, causing deserialization of untrusted phar archive metadata and enabling arbitrary object injection.
